title: Variant Calling Pipeline
description: Learn how to call variants from sequencing data

steps.title: Variant Calling Overview
steps.description:
Variant calling identifies differences between your sample sequences and a reference genome.

This tutorial covers the complete pipeline from raw reads to variant calls.
steps.tip: Variant calling requires a reference genome and quality-controlled reads.

steps.title: Step 1: Quality Control
steps.description:
Before variant calling, assess the quality of your sequencing reads using FastQC or similar tools.

Check for adapter contamination, quality score distribution, and sequence duplication levels.
steps.onExecute: function(value) { return value ? 'Running FastQC on ' + value + '...' : 'Select a FASTQ file'; }
steps.tip: Look for per-base quality scores above Q30 for reliable variant calling.

steps.title: Step 2: Read Alignment
steps.description:
Align reads to the reference genome using BWA-MEM or Bowtie2.

The resulting BAM file contains the genomic positions of each read.
steps.onExecute: function(value) { return value ? 'Aligning to ' + value + ' using BWA-MEM...' : 'Select a reference'; }
steps.tip: Index your reference genome before alignment for faster processing.

steps.title: Step 3: BAM Processing
steps.description:
Process the aligned BAM file:

  • Sort by genomic position
  • Mark duplicate reads
  • Recalibrate base quality scores
steps.tip: GATK's Best Practices recommend BaseRecalibrator for improving variant calling accuracy.

steps.title: Step 4: Variant Calling
steps.description:
Call variants using FreeBayes, GATK HaplotypeCaller, or bcftools.

The output is a VCF file containing all identified variants.
steps.onExecute: function(value) { return value ? 'Calling variants in ' + value + '...' : 'Select a BAM file'; }
steps.tip: Set appropriate quality filters: DP > 10, QUAL > 20, MQ > 40

steps.title: Step 5: Variant Filtering
steps.description:
Filter variants based on quality metrics:

  • Depth of coverage (DP)
  • Mapping quality (MQ)
  • strand bias (SB)
  • Genotype quality (GQ)
steps.tip: Hard filtering is simpler but VQSR (Variant Quality Score Recalibration) provides better results for large datasets.

steps.title: Step 6: Annotation
steps.description:
Annotate variants with functional information using SnpEff or VEP:

  • Consequence (missense, synonymous, etc.)
  • Affected genes
  • Population frequencies
  • Clinical significance
steps.onExecute: function(value) { return value ? 'Annotating ' + value + ' with SnpEff...' : 'Select a VCF file'; }
steps.tip: Download the appropriate genome database before annotation.

steps.title: Results Summary
steps.description:
Your variant calling pipeline is complete! The annotated VCF contains all identified variants with functional predictions.

Review the summary statistics and export results for downstream analysis.
steps.tip: Create a custom workflow combining these steps for reproducible analysis.
